mainEntity.name: What are my rights if I was just walking through the aisles and a forklift backed into me?

acceptedAnswer.text: You have a standard workers\' compensation claim. As a pedestrian, you are generally at no fault, and the focus will be on the operator\'s training and the warehouse\'s safety protocols. These cases often yield high settlements due to the severity of impact injuries.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you were walking and a forklift hit you, you have a very strong workers\' compensation claim because you were a "vulnerable pedestrian" in the workplace. These accidents often involve high-speed impacts or being pinned against a wall or racking. Because you weren\'t the operator, there are fewer "safety violations" the company can try to pin on you.

The investigation will focus on whether the warehouse had designated walking paths and whether the operator was following visibility protocols.

Pedestrian claim focus:
- Was the forklift\'s horn used when coming around corners?
- Did the warehouse have required floor markings and mirrors?
- Was the operator properly certified to drive that specific lift?

Gather information on the warehouse layout. If the company recently removed safety railings or changed flow patterns, that is relevant evidence.

Pedestrian strikes often involve the most severe injuries, and an attorney can ensure the lifelong impact of those injuries is fully compensated.
